frameworks.name: structural neuroimaging / voxel-based morphometry
frameworks.one_line: grey matter volume in rPPC as a structural correlate of risk preference

frameworks.name: economic risk preference / expected utility
frameworks.one_line: tolerance for risky monetary rewards as a behavioral phenotype

frameworks.name: neurodevelopmental aging
frameworks.one_line: age-related cortical atrophy as a mechanism for shifting decision preferences

core_question: Do age-related declines in right posterior parietal cortex grey matter volume account for diminished risk tolerance in older adults better than chronological age?
key_assumptions: rPPC grey matter volume causally relates to individual risk preference
healthy aging entails parietal grey matter loss
risk preference is stably measurable across adulthood
manipulations: participant age range spanning younger to older adults
choices over risky vs. certain rewards
key_findings: older adults show reduced tolerance for risky rewards
rPPC grey matter volume declines with age
rPPC grey matter volume mediates / outperforms age as a predictor of risk preference
interpretations: neuroanatomical decline, not age per se, drives shifts in risky choice across the lifespan
rPPC structure constrains the neural mechanism of value-based decision-making under risk
